concepts.title: Basic Properties of Definite Integrals
concepts.description: The basic properties of definite integrals are: ∫[a, b] f(x) dx = -∫[b, a] f(x) dx, ∫[a, a] f(x) dx = 0, and ∫[a, b] [f(x) + g(x)] dx = ∫[a, b] f(x) dx + ∫[a, b] g(x) dx.

concepts.title: Mean Value Theorem for Integrals
concepts.description: The Mean Value Theorem for Integrals states that if f(x) is a continuous function on the closed interval [a, b], then there exists a point c in [a, b] such that ∫[a, b] f(x) dx = f(c)(b - a).

concepts.title: Definite Integral of Standard Functions
concepts.description: The definite integral of standard functions like ∫[a, b] x^n dx, ∫[a, b] cos(x) dx, ∫[a, b] sin(x) dx, and ∫[a, b] e^x dx can be evaluated using the power rule, trigonometric integrals, and exponential integrals.

concepts.title: Integration of Rational Functions
concepts.description: To integrate rational functions, use the partial fraction decomposition method. Split the rational function into simpler fractions and integrate each fraction separately. Use the formula: ∫[a, b] (ax + b) / (cx^2 + dx + e) dx = (A/x) + (B/2√d) log|2x√(d) + (c)x^2 + (e)| + C.

concepts.title: Incorrect Application of Integration Rules
concepts.description: When integrating functions, remember to check the limits of integration and the constants of integration. Also, be careful when applying the rules of integration, such as the power rule, trigonometric integrals, and exponential integrals.

concepts.title: Units of Integration
concepts.description: When integrating functions, remember that the unit of integration is the same as the unit of the function being integrated. For example, if the function is in meters per second, the unit of integration will be meters.

concepts.title: Integration by Substitution
concepts.description: Integration by substitution is a method of integration where a function is substituted with another function to simplify the integration process. The formula for integration by substitution is: ∫[a, b] f(g(x)) g'(x) dx = ∫[g(a), g(b)] f(u) du.

concepts.title: Integration by Parts
concepts.description: Integration by parts is a method of integration where the product of two functions is integrated. The formula for integration by parts is: ∫[a, b] u dv = uv - ∫[a, b] v du.
